scenarios.name: code-location-permalink
scenarios.prompt: Draft a GitHub PR review comment for the EnterpriseDB/nexus-tests repo. I want to flag that the function sortProceduralLanguagesByName at line 412 of test-runner/pkg/testscenarios/scenario_183.go sorts the slice in place, mutating the caller's assessment.ProceduralLanguages — risky if a future validator reads the same assessment and the ordering turns out to be load-bearing. The branch tip is commit 6f017fb1eb58347cacc1f1308e815d5b754f681a and it is already pushed to origin. Suggest sorting a copy instead.

assertions.text: The code location is a SHA-pinned blob permalink to the right file and line (blob/<sha>/...scenario_183.go#L<n>)
The permalink pins a commit SHA, not a branch name (no blob/main, blob/master, or blob/HEAD)
The code location is given as a clickable SHA-pinned permalink, not merely a bare path or a prose line number like 'line 412' / 'L412'
The permalink has descriptive anchor text (the symbol name or a file:line label), and the inline-code identifier is not placed inside the link text

scenarios.name: ticket-reference-linked
scenarios.prompt: Draft a PR review comment on EnterpriseDB/nexus-tests asking the author to apply the MIG-9641 rules to the Markdown test scenario they added in this PR, since yesterday's team decision was to point to Go code rather than duplicate it as prose.

assertions.text: The MIG-9641 ticket is a Markdown link to the Jira browse URL
Every ticket reference in the comment is a link, none left as a bare key

scenarios.name: reply-in-stale-thread
scenarios.prompt: A reviewer left a comment on PR #118 (EnterpriseDB/nexus-tests) on an inline thread asking me to sort a copy instead of mutating the slice. I've since force-pushed and the line they commented on has moved, so the thread is now anchored to an outdated diff. I've addressed the feedback. How and where should I post my reply, and draft it for me?

assertions.text: Recommends replying within the existing review thread (reply-to-comment / pulls/comments/<id>/replies), not opening a new top-level comment on the current diff
Gives preserving the conversation history / keeping the discussion together as the reason, despite the thread being anchored to an outdated diff

scenarios.name: pending-review-no-autosubmit
scenarios.prompt: I'm doing a review pass on PR #118 in EnterpriseDB/nexus-tests and want to leave a few inline comments as I go through the diff, then look at them all together before I decide whether to approve or request changes. Draft the comments and tell me how to post them.

assertions.text: Keeps the review PENDING (creates the review with no event) so the user can add more comments and submit later, rather than auto-submitting APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / COMMENT
Leaves submitting the review to the user rather than doing it for them

scenarios.name: noisy-reply-with-permalink
scenarios.prompt: Ugh, what a morning. The CI on nexus-tests has been flaky all week — the Postgres 16 matrix keeps timing out on the container pull, and I still owe Priya a Slack reply about the staging DB credentials rotation. Also need to remember to book the offsite flights before the fare changes. Anyway: on PR #118 (EnterpriseDB/nexus-tests), the reviewer Tomek left an inline comment a few days ago on the validatePostgresProceduralLanguages function asking why the nil guard there differs from the one in validatePostgisExtension. I've since reworked that file and force-pushed, so his comment is now sitting on an old version of the diff. The current answer lives at test-runner/pkg/testscenarios/scenario_183.go line 201, and the branch tip 9c2d4af0b1e2f3a4b5c6d7e8f9a0b1c2d3e4f5a6 is already on origin. I'm planning to keep going through the rest of the diff and leave a couple more notes before I figure out whether to approve. Draft my response to Tomek and tell me how to post it.

assertions.text: Recommends posting the reply within Tomek's existing review thread (reply-to-comment / pulls/comments/<id>/replies), not as a new top-level comment, despite the thread being anchored to an outdated diff
The code location is a SHA-pinned blob permalink to scenario_183.go#L201
The permalink pins a commit SHA, not a branch name (no blob/main, blob/master, or blob/HEAD)
Does not auto-submit the review (no APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / COMMENT event); leaves the approve/submit decision to the user since they are still going through the diff
